author: @DiyEcoProjects
text:
Hi robert, things good mate? hope so... ive bought some of your paint and thought to give this a go. Will share the results with you.

Im thinking of making a small 12v one, with a step up voltage regulator.... Would this be the way to go, a "step up" rather than a "pwm" 12v step down-er.
 
Since writing this, ive bought both. so will see what they do lol

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: lol - to be honest mate - i always think the best answers are found by trying - looking forward to seeing the vids

yep :o)

im making it for my friend who needs heater for his live i van. thats why we bought it. will share - thanks

author: @helpmonkey
text: hello Robert ... I am from Canada and operate the website greenwatts.info ... my site is basically a DIY for green energy projects ... I design and build the project then publish the design and construction on the web site ... I would very much like to do a project with your heat ink. I would be glad to link back to your vidoes and website. What would be the best way for me to obtain enough for a project to construct radiant floor heat for a 12 ft by 12 ft room.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: are you after a freebie in return for publicity?

replies.author: @helpmonkey
replies.text: Robert Murray-Smith well I am open to any ideas ... I am in the process of re-expanding my website ... right now ad revenues are a few hundred dollars a month so I don't have a lot of spare cash. I am sure with my subject matter and draw greenwatts.info could provide some benefit to your company. How much ink would you recommend I need enough for some experimentation (which I could document) and the room project.

replies.author: @helpmonkey
replies.text: Robert Murray-Smith I think I could get away with 50ml to start with ... does your contact in Ontario Canada have some of this in stock?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text:
helpmonkey
he should have and 50mL sounds like a good start
